key_risks: Weak June payrolls or hot wage print could trigger rate/risk repricing across cyclicals
Reversal in healthcare/biotech after strong 30d run (momentum reversal risk)
Mega-cap tech rebound would cause equal-weight and non-tech tilt to lag SPY benchmark
FOMC minutes July 8 could surprise hawkish given sticky 3.4% core PCE

portfolio.rationale: Strong recent leadership (+8.4% 30d, +9.8% vs SPY) with defensive characteristics ahead of jobs data and holiday-shortened week; low beta cushions downside.
Broadening rally beneficiary with steady momentum (+6.9% 30d); benefits from firm rates and upcoming bank earnings positioning.
ISM manufacturing still expansionary at 53.3 with new orders 56.0; industrials showing consistent breadth (+6.6% 30d).
Strongest 30d momentum (+17.3%) with catalyst-rich window; supported by broader healthcare leadership.
Diversified market breadth exposure that has outperformed cap-weighted SPY, hedging against mega-cap tech weakness noted in briefing.

portfolio_rationale: Tilted toward defensive/broadening leaders (healthcare, financials, industrials) given softening tech (Micron, AI names weak), sticky core PCE at 3.4%, and jobs report risk on July 2. Equal-weight SPX captures breadth without mega-cap tech concentration risk.
rationale_summary: Overweight sectors showing durable breadth and defensive tilt into a holiday-shortened week with payrolls catalyst; underweight mega-cap tech given recent AI-name weakness and elevated volatility.
